2604.06758.title: Multilingual Cognitive Impairment Detection in the Era of Foundation Models
2604.06758.paper_summary: We evaluate cognitive impairment (CI) classification from transcripts of speech in English, Slovene, and Korean. We compare zero-shot large language models (LLMs) used as direct classifiers under three input settings -- transcript-only, linguistic-features-only, and combined -- with supervised tabular approaches trained under a leave-one-out protocol. The tabular models operate on engineered linguistic features, transcript embeddings, and early or late fusion of both modalities. Across languages, zero-shot LLMs provide competitive no-training baselines, but supervised tabular models generally perform better, particularly when engineered linguistic features are included and combined with embeddings. Few-shot experiments focusing on embeddings indicate that the value of limited supervision is language-dependent, with some languages benefiting substantially from additional labelled examples while others remain constrained without richer feature representations. Overall, the results suggest that, in small-data CI detection, structured linguistic signals and simple fusion-based classifiers remain strong and reliable signals.
2604.06758.paper_summary_zh: 我們評估來自英語、斯洛維尼亞語和韓語的語音轉錄中的認知障礙（CI）分類。我們比較了在三種輸入設置下用作直接分類器的零樣本大型語言模型（LLMs）——僅轉錄、僅語言特徵和結合——與在留一法協議下訓練的監督表格方法。表格模型基於工程化的語言特徵、轉錄嵌入，以及早期或晚期融合這兩種模式。在不同語言中，零樣本LLMs提供了具有競爭力的無訓練基準，但監督表格模型通常表現更好，特別是在包含工程化語言特徵並與嵌入結合時。專注於嵌入的少樣本實驗表明，有限監督的價值依賴於語言，某些語言從額外的標記示例中獲益顯著，而其他語言則在缺乏更豐富的特徵表示時受到限制。總體而言，結果表明，在小數據CI檢測中，結構化的語言信號和基於簡單融合的分類器仍然是強大且可靠的信號。
2604.06758.author: Damar Hoogland et.al.
2604.06758.authors: Damar Hoogland, Boshko Koloski, Jaya Caporusso, Tine Kolenik, Ana Zwitter Vitez, Senja Pollak, Christina Manouilidou, Matthew Purver
